mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already below its recent peak, discount-rate pressure on equities eases and the market assigns a higher present value to future earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during periods of declining FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A high or rising FEDFUNDS rate above the neutral range tightens financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month once policy is restrictive enough.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the sign of the relation reverses in a sustained 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor income and lower expected household spending, which should translate into weaker equity returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if labor-market deterioration events defined by the trigger are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the effect is absent in a rolling 4-quarter test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating PAYEMS growth signals payroll expansion and stronger aggregate income, which should support near-term equity performance through better demand and earnings momentum over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS growth does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the rolling Rank IC is below 0.03 for any 3-year span.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS improve together, stronger retail sales are more likely to reflect real income-supported demand rather than price effects, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month via earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of rising RSAFS and rising PAYEMS does not outperform each series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO together with firm HOUST weakness signals a real activity slowdown in production and construction, which should lead equity investors to mark down cyclical earnings and push returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns better than either series individually, or if the combined signal flips positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS is also improving, the economy is expanding on both broad output and consumption dimensions, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint strength in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not yield positive 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction coefficient is not statistically positive in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary tightening phase, a stable or falling CPIAUCSL combined with high FEDFUNDS lowers expected real financing pressure and supports equities; if CPI inflation re-accelerates, the same rate level becomes restrictive and the signal fails.
failure_condition: Reject if low or falling CPIAUCSL plus high FEDFUNDS does not improve 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the sign turns negative when inflation is stable or rising.
